title: M5 Complex Heat-Sink Solver-Backed D1 Transfer Gate Results
description: This report summarizes more complex solver-backed heat-sink D1 label-scarcity transfer runs comparing scratch, Thermal GeoPT pretraining, and the original GeoPT checkpoint.
interpretation_rule: A positive M5 signal means a pretrained group improves scratch by about 10% relative L2 at 25 or 50 labels and does not degrade max-temperature or hotspot metrics. Original GeoPT is the primary cross-physics transfer control.
